goal: Make ltm-example's email.md exciting enough that a developer would forward it to a teammate.
success_criteria: email.md is under 200 words and reads aloud without stumbling.
A reader who has never heard of ltm can explain what it does after one pass.
The rewrite leads with the pain (re-briefing a fresh agent on a new machine) before naming the tool.
No corporate hedging: phrases like 'may be of interest', 'serves as', or 'utility for the transfer of' are gone.
constraints: Keep it a single email.md file — no new files, no folder restructure.
Do not invent features ltm does not have. Verify against ltm's SPEC.md before writing.

decisions.what: Output stays in email.md, not a redesigned README or landing page.
decisions.why: The repo's job is to be a fixed target for the 'ltm example' command — diverging from a single email.md breaks the demo's contract.
decisions.consequences: Suggestions to add a README, GIF, or marketing site belong in a separate ltm repo PR, not here.

decisions.what: Keep the file format as plain Markdown.
decisions.why: The agent rewriting it can be any model; assuming HTML or MJML breaks portability of the demo.

methods.name: voice-test-read-aloud
methods.when_applicable: Polishing short-form copy where flow matters more than density.
methods.how: Read the draft aloud once. Cut anything you stumble on. Re-read. Cut again. Stop when one full pass reads cleanly without backtracking.

methods.name: lead-with-pain
methods.when_applicable: Writing intro copy for a developer tool.
methods.how: Open with the concrete moment the reader has lived through (e.g., re-briefing a fresh agent at midnight). Name the tool only after the pain lands.

attempts.tried: Drafted a version that opened with 'ltm is a portable memory protocol for AI agents.'
attempts.learned: Leading with a category label loses the reader before the value lands — the pain has to come first.

open_questions: Should the rewrite include a one-liner install command, or stay purely narrative?
Is it worth showing a tiny example packet inline, or does that bloat past the 200-word budget?
next_step: Clone github.com/dennisdevulder/ltm-example and rewrite email.md to read like a developer recommending the tool to a friend.
